Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.reasonerExtensionTests

Class AbstractReasonerTests

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.reasonerExtensionTests.AbstractReasonerTests
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractAutomaticReasonerTests, AbstractManualReasonerTests, AbstrExprTests, AllDTests, AllITests, AllmpDTests, AllmtDTests, ConcreteReasonerTestsExample, ConjFTests, ContrHypsTests, ContrTests, CutTests, DisjETests, EhTests, EqvLRTests, EqvRLTests, ExETests, ExFTests, FunImgSimpRewriterTests, GeneralizedModusPonensTests, HeTests, HypOrTests, HypTests, ImpCaseTests, ImpETests, IsFunGoalTests, LocalEqRewriteTests, MapOvrGoalTests, MembershipGoalTests, ModusTollensTests, NegEnumTests, OnePointRuleTests, ReasonerExtensionTests
public abstract class AbstractReasonerTests extends Object
An abstract class that can be extended in order to be used to test reasoner extensions.

Tests performed include :

  • Registry entry tests
  • Reasoner failure tests
  • Reasoner success tests (including input serialization tests, replay tests, and optional logical justification tests)

Since Rodin 3.3, to write tests for a new reasoner, one has to create test methods that call for each test case either methods:

  • #assertReasonerSuccess()
  • #assertReasonerFailure()

For Rodin 3.2, one used to write tests for a new reasoner by calling the two methods:

  • #testSuccessfulReasonerApplications()
  • #testUnsuccessfulReasonerApplications()
passing them the appropriate test cases.

Prior to version 3.2, the tests used to be implemented by overriding the methods:

  • getSuccessfulReasonerApplications()
  • #getUnsuccessfulReasonerApplications()
However, this made the tests difficult to debug as there was no indication of the location of a failing test. This is why the interface for writing tests has evolved in version 3.2. Nevertheless, we retain backward compatibility with the tests written with this old interface. The change for Rodin 3.3 still maintains backward compatibility but allows for localized tests (one can read the location of the test that failed from the stack trace).

Since:
3.2, 3.3

    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static interface 
    AbstractReasonerTests.IRulePatcher
    Protocol for modifying a proof rule for the replay tests.
    static class 
    AbstractReasonerTests.SuccessfullReasonerApplication
    This class contains the inputs to a successfull call to the IReasoner#apply() method and its expected result.
    static class 
    AbstractReasonerTests.UnsuccessfullReasonerApplication
    This class contains the inputs to an unsuccessfull call to the IReasoner#apply() method and its expected result.

    • getReasonerID

      public abstract String getReasonerID()
      Returns the reasoner id of the reasoner to test
      Returns:
      the reasoner id of the reasoner to test

    • getSuccessfulReasonerApplications

      @Deprecated public AbstractReasonerTests.SuccessfullReasonerApplication[] getSuccessfulReasonerApplications()
      Deprecated.
      Do not override this method anymore. Call testSuccessfulReasonerApplications(String, SuccessfullReasonerApplication...) with your test cases instead.
      Returns the successful reasoner applications to test
      Returns:
      the successful reasoner applications to test

    • assertReasonerFailure

      public void assertReasonerFailure(IProverSequent sequent, IReasonerInput input, String reason) throws UntranslatableException
      Tests the current reasoner for failure. The reasoner to test is specified by getReasonerID(). This method runs the IReasoner#apply() method of the reasoner and checks its return value. This is performed in various situations to ensure that the reasoner implements the common reasoner specification.
      Parameters:
      sequent - the input sequent
      input - the reasoner input
      reason - the expected reason for reasoner failure, or null in case this check should not be performed.
      Throws:
      UntranslatableException
      See Also:
      • getReasonerID()

    • assertReasonerFailure

      public void assertReasonerFailure(String sequentImage, IReasonerInput input, String reason) throws UntranslatableException
      Shortcut for {@link #assertReasonerFailure(IProverSequent, IReasonerInput, String).
      Parameters:
      sequentImage - the input sequent to be parsed with TestLib.genSeq(String)
      input - the reasoner input
      reason - expected error, or null
      Throws:
      UntranslatableException

    • assertReasonerSuccess

      public void assertReasonerSuccess(IProverSequent sequent, IReasonerInput input, AbstractReasonerTests.IRulePatcher rulePatcher, IProverSequent... newSequents) throws UntranslatableException
      Tests the current reasoner for success. The reasoner to test is specified by getReasonerID(). This method runs the IReasoner#apply() method of the reasoner and checks its return value, including its application to proof trees. This is performed in various situations to ensure that the reasoner implements the common reasoner specification.
      Parameters:
      sequent - the input sequent
      input - the reasoner input
      rulePatcher - the rule patcher to use for replay checks
      newSequents - the new sequents obtained by applying the rule generated by the reasoner application on the given sequent, or null in case this check should not be performed
      Throws:
      UntranslatableException
      See Also:
      • getReasonerID()

    • assertReasonerSuccess

      public void assertReasonerSuccess(IProverSequent sequent, IReasonerInput input, IProverSequent... newSequents) throws UntranslatableException
      Shortcut for {@link #assertReasonerSuccess(IProverSequent, IReasonerInput, IRulePatcher, IProverSequent...).
      Parameters:
      sequent - the input sequent
      input - the reasoner input
      newSequentImages - the new sequents
      Throws:
      UntranslatableException

    • assertReasonerSuccess

      public void assertReasonerSuccess(IProverSequent sequent, IReasonerInput input, String... newSequentImages) throws UntranslatableException
      Shortcut for {@link #assertReasonerSuccess(IProverSequent, IReasonerInput, IProverSequent...).
      Parameters:
      sequent - the input sequent
      input - the reasoner input
      newSequentImages - the new sequents in string format, or null
      Throws:
      UntranslatableException

      public void assertReasonerSuccess(String sequentImage, IReasonerInput input, String... newSequents) throws UntranslatableException

    • getUnsuccessfullReasonerApplications

      @Deprecated public AbstractReasonerTests.UnsuccessfullReasonerApplication[] getUnsuccessfullReasonerApplications()
      Deprecated.
      Do not override this method anymore. Call testUnsuccessfulReasonerApplications(String, UnsuccessfullReasonerApplication...) with your test cases instead.
      Returns the unsuccessful reasoner applications to test
      Returns:
      the unsuccessful reasoner applications to test

    • getJustDischTactic

      public ITactic getJustDischTactic()
      Returns the tactic that should be used to test that the justifications for the rules generated by the reasoner are valid.

      By default this method returns null and rule justifications are not tested for validity. Clients that wish to do this may override this method with their tactic of choice.

      This method of testing generated proof rules for validity is intended to be used more as logical debugging support than to be used in regular reasoner unit tests.

      Returns:
      the tactic to use to discharge the justifications of all rules created by this test case.

    • setUp

      public void setUp() throws Exception
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testReasonerRegistryEntry

      public final void testReasonerRegistryEntry()
      Tests that the entry for the reasoner in the reasoner registry is correct.

    • testReasonerFailure

      @Deprecated public final void testReasonerFailure()
      Deprecated.
      Use testUnsuccessfulReasonerApplications(String, UnsuccessfullReasonerApplication...) instead.
      Tests the correct failure of the reasoner.

    • testReasonerSuccess

      @Deprecated public final void testReasonerSuccess() throws Exception
      Deprecated.
      Use testSuccessfulReasonerApplications(String, SuccessfullReasonerApplication...) instead.
      Tests the correct success of the reasoner and the rule generated by it.
      Throws:
      Exception
